html:
Exclusive to:

For sculptures that can be crafted by players on a Potter's Wheel, see Chess Pieces.
For decorative sculptures dropping Sketches when mined, see Marble Statues.

Incomplete Rook SculptureIncomplete Bishop SculptureIncomplete Knight Sculpture

The Marble Sculptures are naturally spawning structures in Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. They spawn in some Set Pieces that can be found anywhere in the world, alongside several Marble Statues.

The player can mine the Marble Sculptures with a Pickaxe, causing them to drop Marbles and become broken forms that look like Clockwork Monsters. They can be repaired by their respective Suspicious Marbles which can be found anywhere in the world.

The repaired Marble Sculptures can be mined with a Pickaxe at Full Moon or New Moon. At Full Moon, each repaired Sculpture can be mined to drop a Sketch and spawn its corresponding Clockwork Bishop, Clockwork Knight, or Clockwork Rook. Alternatively, when mined at New Moon, each repaired sculpture drops a Sketch and Gears, and spawns Shadow Pieces. Reanimating the Sculptures during either moon phase will unlock the respective Chess Figures to drop in Tumbleweeds.

Since the Sculptures are not renewable, if players want to spawn more Shadow Pieces, they have to craft Chess Pieces with the Sketches dropped from Marble Sculptures.

If Sculptures are mined when not in Full Moon or New Moon, they will simply break and drop the Suspicious Marbles from Marble Sculptures.

Trivia

  • Marble Sculptures were added in the A Little Fixer Upper update.

Gallery

  • A mined Marble Rook.
  • A repaired Marble Rook with its nose.
  • A mined Marble Bishop.
  • repaired Marble Bishop with its head.
  • A mined Marble Knight.
  • A repaired Marble Knight with its head.
  • Wilson standing in a Marble Sculptures setpiece.
  • Marble Sculptures on full moon.
  • The setpiece on the map.
  • Marble Sculpture as seen in the poster for the A Little Fixer Upper update.
  • Marble Sculptures as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
  • A set piece with 2 Knights.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

"sculpture_rookbody"

"sculpture_knightbody"
Incomplete Rook SculptureIncomplete Bishop SculptureIncomplete Knight Sculpture
“It's not in the best shape.”
–Wilson, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“What a creepy load of junk!”
–Willow, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Is many big rocks!”
–Wolfgang, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Perhaps it wanted to hide the shame of its monstrous form?”
–Wendy, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“PATTERN RECOGNITION: FAILED”
–WX-78, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“What an awful state it's in!”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Looks a bit like one of them chargin' hosers.”
–Woodie, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Why would anyone want to cover this up?”
–Maxwell, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“I fear we've unleashed a great evil.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“It was hiding!”
–Webber, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Where is your nose?”
–Warly, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Needs fixing”
–Wormwood, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“I could probably fix that up a bit.”
–Winona, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Look! It's a rook!”
–Wortox, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“Missing something...”
–Wurt, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“There's something missing...”
–Walter, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“It still doesn't look right.”
–Wanda, when examining an incomplete Rook Sculpture.
“There's a big piece missing.”
–Wilson, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“The crank doesn't even turn!”
–Willow, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Strange little man was in big rock!”
–Wolfgang, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“The more the marble wastes, the more the statue grows.”
–Wendy, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“DETACHED HEADS UPSET THE FLESHLINGS. HAHA”
–WX-78, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“So this is what was lurking inside.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Creepy.”
–Woodie, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Come now! I liked that one.”
–Maxwell, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“We've freed the figure slumbering in the stöne.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Where's your head, mister?”
–Webber, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“It looks incomplete.”
–Warly, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Broken?”
–Wormwood, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Needs a proper repair job.”
–Winona, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Time to fix-up this here bish-up.”
–Wortox, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“It lost its head, flurp!”
–Wurt, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Can you sniff out the missing piece Woby?”
–Walter, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“Either the sculptor forgot something, or a piece has been removed.”
–Wanda, when examining an incomplete Bishop Sculpture.
“I guess he cracked under the pressure.”
–Wilson, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“That thing looks terrible.”
–Willow, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“Hidden marble man has big booboo.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“We all have monsters within, I suppose.”
–Wendy, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“SOME ASSEMBLY REQUIRED”
–WX-78, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“They say every block of stone has a statue inside.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“Yikes. Put it back in.”
–Woodie, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“My creations! How rude.”
–Maxwell, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“An icön öf evil, surely!”
–Wigfrid, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“Peekaboo, monster!”
–Webber, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“It's looking for its missing piece.”
–Warly, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“Needs help?”
–Wormwood, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“Creepy. Let's fix it.”
–Winona, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“That's a knight alright!”
–Wortox, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“It broken!”
–Wurt, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“It's all broken up.”
–Walter, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.
“It's a little better, but there's something missing...”
–Wanda, when examining an incomplete Knight Sculpture.

The player can mine the Marble Sculptures with a [[Pickaxe]], causing them to drop [[Marble]]s and become broken forms that look like [[:Category:Clockwork Monsters|Clockwork Monsters]]. At Full Moon, each repaired Sculpture can be mined to drop a [[Sketch]] and spawn its corresponding [[Clockwork Bishop]], [[Clockwork Knight]], or [[Clockwork Rook]]. Alternatively, when mined at New Moon, each repaired sculpture drops a Sketch and [[Gear]]s, and spawns [[Shadow Pieces]]. Reanimating the Sculptures during either moon phase will unlock the respective [[Chess Figures]] to drop in [[Tumbleweed]]s. Since the Sculptures are not renewable, if players want to spawn more Shadow Pieces, they have to craft [[Chess Pieces]] with the Sketches dropped from Marble Sculptures. If Sculptures are mined when not in Full Moon or New Moon, they will simply break and drop the Suspicious Marbles from Marble Sculptures. {{Clear}} == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * Marble Sculptures were added in the ''[[A New Reign#September 1, 2016 - A Little Fixer Upper|A Little Fixer Upper]]'' update. =={{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Marble Rook.png|A mined Marble Rook.
